name: TAO Reset Pullback Persistence v1
edge_mechanism: trend resumption after a deeper pullback resets momentum without fully breaking medium-term structure
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing whether deeper pullbacks can reset momentum and then resume once directional pressure reasserts through medium-term structure. The edge is not shallow EMA-touch continuation; it is buying or selling the reset after enough adverse movement has already washed out weak positioning.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reset pullbacks remain one-bar wonders, if TAO keeps mean-reverting instead of persisting after reset, or if structure references are too loose and produce anti-trend churn.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A strong move cools off through a deeper pullback, momentum recenters, structure holds, and directional pressure returns for another leg in the original direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Volatile assets like TAO often need a larger reset before trend participants can re-engage. If the pullback is deep enough to shake out weak positions but not deep enough to truly break structure, the next impulse can be more tradeable than the initial move.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because the asset tends to overshoot enough for genuine reset behavior, while 4h keeps the pullback visible instead of devolving into micro-noise.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: required pullback depth and stop normalizer
indicator_role_map.RSI_14: momentum reset detector rather than overbought/oversold label
indicator_role_map.PLUS_DI_14/MINUS_DI_14: persistence confirmation once directional pressure returns

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The pullback may be deep because trend is actually broken, causing persistence entries to fire into a fresh reversal rather than a resumed trend.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If signals are dense but too noisy, tighten the structure hold around EMA_55 before changing pullback depth.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Fewer trades than pure mean reversion but larger average winners when persistence reasserts after a real reset.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO shows repeated reset-then-persist sequences with credible density, and ETH or DOGE confirms the mechanism in a milder form.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or repeated entries that reveal the reset was actually structural breakdown.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low; deep pullback, structure hold, momentum recentering, and DI persistence are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: higher than range reacceptance but materially broader than the killed shallow-reset families because pullback depth and persistence thresholds are looser and role-separated

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing whether deeper resets on a volatile asset produce a cleaner second impulse.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO shows real reset depth followed by directional persistence and non-trivial follow-through.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO keeps turning reset entries into reversals or chop.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: A moderate number of larger continuation moves after deeper resets.

validation_basket.why_included: Verifies whether the same reset-persistence idea survives on a cleaner, lower-beta lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows the same sequence with smaller but cleaner follow-through.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The thesis is only volatility-dependent and disappears on ETH.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer, cleaner reset continuations than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism works on a meme-driven expressive lane with different crowding behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE preserves the reset-persistence sequence with enough density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE mean-reverts too hard for persistence after reset.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More false starts than ETH but enough second-leg moves to validate the thesis.
